data.title: Is weakening jobs market a product of uncertainty, AI, or both?
data.description:
Key points:
  • S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow all red; Nasdaq down most
  • Tech biggest loser among S&P sectors; Cons staples leads gainers
  • STOXX down 0.5%
  • Dollar up; gold, crude down; bitcoin off >3%
  • US 10-year Treasury yields rise to 4.19%

IS WEAKENING JOBS MARKET A PRODUCT OF UNCERTAINTY, AI, OR BOTH?

This week Powell & Co cut interest rates, largely because of signs of softening in the labor market.

But with current official jobs data unavailable at the time of its meeting, the Fed has had to make do with unofficial, alternative data sources.

Two of those alternative data sources - The National Federation of Independent Business (NFIB) and The Conference Board - appear to support the Fed's case.

The graphic below pits NFIB's uncertainty index against the Conference Board's "jobs hard to find" component. To state the obvious, business uncertainty is elevated and workers are finding it harder to find gigs.

But NFIB's chief economist Bill Dunkelberg attributes heightened uncertainty to capital expenditure plans and says "job growth continues to be constrained by a lack of qualified employees."

In fact, scarcity of qualified workers edged out inflation as the "most important problem," in NFIB's most recent Business Optimism report.

As for consumers, Dana Peterson, chief economist at The Conference Board writes, "the share of consumers who say jobs are 'plentiful' minus the share saying 'hard to get'—dipped again in November after a brief respite in October from its year-to-date decline," adding that "consumers were notably more pessimistic about business conditions six months from now."

According to yet another independent data source, Challenger, Gray & Christmas, "so far this year, AI is responsible for 54,694 layoff plans," the executive outplacement firm says in its November planned layoffs report. "Since 2023, when this reason was first cited, AI has led to 71,683 job cut announcements."

So is this a skills gap story?

"Technology and innovation move at a faster pace than education and jobs training," Oliver Pursche, senior vice president at Wealthspire Advisors tells Reuters. "When the automobile was first invented, there's probably a huge shortage of mechanics."

"It is absolutely correct to infer from the data that there's a combination of reduced hiring/increased layoffs related to both the uncertainty and the benefits - the productivity improvements related to AI," Pursche adds.

data.title: Coty dips after report says CEO, chair may leave as shareholder JAB plans leadership change
data.description:
** Shares of CoverGirl parent Coty down about 2% at $3.27

** Co's controlling shareholder JAB Holding plans a leadership overhaul, resulting in CEO Sue Nabi stepping down, the Financial Times reports, citing people familiar with the matter

** Move comes as Coty is mulling the sale of its cosmetics business to focus on more profitable fragrance segment

** Athletic apparel maker Lululemon Athletica on Thursday announced CEO Calvin McDonald's exit in January after about seven years at the helm

** Coty and JAB did not immediately respond to Reuters request for comment

** As of last close, stock down 52% YTD

data.title: European prices rise in afternoon trade on cooler weather outlook
data.description:
Dutch and British gas prices rose on Friday afternoon on expectations of higher demand.

The benchmark Dutch front-month contract at the TTF hub (TRNLTTFMc1) was up 0.73 euro at 27.65 euros per megawatt hour (MWh), or $9.51/mmBtu, by 1627 GMT, data compiled by LSEG showed.

The Dutch day-ahead contract (TRNLTTFD1) was up 0.58 euro at 27.58 euros/MWh.

The British front-month gas prices (TRGBNBPMc1) was up by 2.66 pence at 73.31 pence per therm, while the day-ahead contract (TRGBNBPD1) was up by 2.56 at 71.31 p/therm.

Local distribution zone (LDZ) demand, which largely reflects heating demand, is set to rise on Monday as temperatures adjust lower towards the seasonal norm, LSEG analyst Saku Jussila said.

While this supported prices, gains were likely to be limited, he said.

Flows of liquefied natural gas (LNG) into northwest Europe slowed in December as milder weather has curbed demand, but low gas storage levels will mean LNG arrivals will remain high, Daniel Hynes, senior commodity strategist at ANZ, said.

EU gas storage sites were last 71.29% full, compared with 80.89% at the same time last year, Gas Infrastructure Europe data showed.

In the European carbon market, the benchmark contract (CFI2Zc1) was down 0.20 euro at 83.70 euros ($98.17) a metric ton.

The contract hit an 11-month high of 84.40 euros/ton on Thursday on expectations of tighter supply in 2026, LSEG analyst Agnieszka Wasylewicz said.

($1 = 0.8526 euros)

data.title: Furniture retailer RH rises after Q3 revenue beat
data.description:
** Shares of luxury furniture retailer RH rise nearly 6% to about $162.7

** On Thursday, co beat third-quarter revenue estimates despite worsening housing market and polarizing tariff impacts

** RH reported Q3 revenue of 883.81 million, slightly above analysts' estimate of $883.69 million — data compiled by LSEG

** However, co posted Q3 adj. EPS of $1.71 vs. Wall Street's estimate of $2.16

** RH sees FY25 revenue growth in the range of 9% to 9.2%

** Six of 20 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, 11 "hold" and three "sell"; their median PT is $188

** Including session's moves, shares down 58.6% YTD

data.title: Broadcom's Margins Expected to Narrow Due to AI Revenue — Market Talk
data.description: Broadcom is expecting AI to squeeze its gross profit margins in 2026, Truist analyst William Stein says. New orders for AI server racks come with significantly lower margins than is typical for the chipmaker, Stein says. Based on Broadcom's comments Thursday, Stein now estimates total gross profit margins will be around 74% in 2026, down from 78.6% in 2025 and below the 77.1% Stein was previously projecting. "We think the higher EPS overwhelms the GPM impact, but we recognize this metric is critical for many semi investors," he says. Shares fall 11%. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: Supertanker Skipper seized by US near Venezuela is heading to Houston, sources say
data.description:
By Jonathan Saul, Marianna Parraga and Arathy Somasekhar

The oil supertanker Skipper that was seized by the U.S. near Venezuela this week as part of an increased pressure strategy against President Nicolas Maduro, is heading to Houston, two sources said on Friday.

The Very Large Crude Carrier, which is carrying about 1.85 million barrels of Venezuela's Merey heavy crude according to satellite images analyzed by TankerTrackers.com, is too large to enter the Houston port and will need to anchor nearby and offload the cargo to smaller ships, the sources added.

The Houston-Galveston sector of the U.S. Coast Guard and Port Houston did not immediately reply to requests for comment. Venezuela's state oil company PDVSA, the cargo's seller, did not reply to a request for comment.

U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi said this week the tanker was intercepted and retained under a seizure warrant. Guyana's maritime authority said it was falsely flying the country's flag.

The seizure of the sanctioned oil tanker has sharply escalated tensions between Washington and Caracas. Washington is preparing to intercept more ships transporting Venezuelan oil, sources familiar with the matter said on Thursday.

data.title: Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend and Extends Share Repurchase Authorization
data.description:
(16:49 GMT) *Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend And Extends Share Repurchase Authorization >VAC
(16:49 GMT) Press Release: Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend and Extends Share Repurchase Authorization

ORLANDO, Fla.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--December 12, 2025--

Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation (the "Company") today announced that its Board of Directors increased its quarterly dividend to $0.80 per share of common stock. The dividend is payable on or around January 7, 2026, to stockholders of record as of the close of business on December 24, 2025.

In addition, the Board of Directors extended the Company's share repurchase authorization through December 31, 2026.

Share repurchases may be made through open-market purchases, privately negotiated transactions, block transactions, tender offers, or otherwise. The specific timing, amount, and other terms of the repurchases will depend on market conditions, corporate and regulatory requirements, contractual restrictions, and other factors. In connection with the share repurchase program, the Company is authorized to adopt one or more plans pursuant to the provisions of Rule 10b5-1 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The authorization for the share repurchase program may be suspended, terminated, increased or decreased by the Board at any time without prior notice.

About Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation

Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation is a leading global vacation company that offers vacation ownership, exchange, rental and resort and property management, along with related businesses, products, and services. The Company has 120 vacation ownership resorts and approximately 700,000 owner families in a diverse portfolio that includes some of the most iconic vacation ownership brands. The Company also operates an exchange network and membership programs comprised of more than 3,200 affiliated resorts in over 90 countries and territories, and provides management services to other resorts and lodging properties. As a leader and innovator in the vacation industry, the Company upholds the highest standards of excellence in serving its customers, investors and associates while maintaining exclusive, long-term relationships with Marriott International, Inc. and an affiliate of Hyatt Hotels Corporation for the development, sales and marketing of vacation ownership products and services. For more information, please visit www.marriottvacationsworldwide.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251212583528/en/

CONTACT: Neal Goldner

Investor Relations

Investor@mvwc.com

Cameron Klaus

Global Communications

407-206-6300

data.title: ICE Canola: Weaker Outside Markets, Profit-Taking Pulling
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were lower mid-morning Friday, due to losses in outside markets and from profit-taking said a trader.

The trader said the vegetable oils were the biggest influence on canola, with declines in Chicago soy, MATIF rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil. Crude oil was stepping back as well, which added more pressure on the veg oils.

Also, he said canola for most of this week approached some resistance levels as traders covered export business. That has come to an end and he expects activity to wind down as Christmas approaches.

At 289,200 tons, exports of canola improved over the week ended Dec. 7, the Canadian Grain Commission reported. That brought the 2025/26 cumulative total to 2.38 million tons, compared with 4.03 million a year ago.

The Canadian dollar eased back by mid-session Friday, with the loonie at 72.51 U.S. cents, compared with Thursday's close of 72.60.

Approximately 45,100 canola contracts were traded as of 11:31 a.m. EST, with prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton:

 
           Price      Change 
Jan       610.20    dn 11.10 
Mar       622.60    dn 10.20 
May       634.50     dn 9.50 
Jul       642.30     dn 8.60

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Historic Flooding in Washington Displaces Thousands of Residents — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph De Avila

Catastrophic flooding has swept through Washington, prompting tens of thousands to evacuate after a storm dropped more than a foot of rain on parts of the western portion of the state.

More than 300 Washington National Guard members have been mobilized and have carried out water rescues. Washington Gov. Bob Ferguson has declared a state of emergency.

Skagit County officials on Friday urged all residents of Burlington, a city with a population of nearly 10,000 located about 70 miles north of Seattle, to evacuate their homes immediately after floodwaters began inundating houses in the area. The National Guard will be going door to door to notify residents and to assist with evacuations, county officials said.

Forecasts show another storm coming Monday and could add another 2 to 3 inches of rain in the Olympic Mountains and North Cascades in western Washington. Several rivers including the Snohomish River and Skagit River are running very high and continue to be near or above record stage, posing major threats for residents, said Kirby Cook, a science and operations officer for the National Weather Service in Seattle.

"It's going to take a while for a lot of this water to run off," Cook said. "Any precipitation that starts to fall again is going to be a concern because it's going to add to the flows on the rivers again."

Everson, a small city in Whatcom County located about 100 miles north of Seattle, was hit by heavy flood water. Videos on social media and verified by Storyful, which is owned by News Corp, the parent company of The Wall Street Journal, show the community inundated with water and nearly covering an 8-foot tall fence. Another video released by the Coast Guard shows a helicopter rescuing residents who became trapped in their attic in Sumas, Wash.

Floodwaters covered streets and closed businesses in Sumas, Nooksack and Marietta, according to Whatcom County officials.

Write to Joseph De Avila at joseph.deavila@wsj.com

data.title: Lululemon's Turnaround Timing Unclear Amid CEO Search — Market Talk
data.description: Lululemon's plans to search for new leadership makes the timing of a durable turnaround unclear, Raymond James analysts say in a note. If or when a new chief executive is named, it will likely take time for that person to get up to speed and implement their strategy, the analysts say. "This suggests any significant change at LULU is likely to be an out-year event, in our view," they say. For now, Lululemon lacks any catalyst until new products hit in the spring, and how it will resonate with customers remains a gamble, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Incyte Corp Announces Board Retirement
data.description:
On December 10, 2025, Incyte Corporation announced the immediate retirement of Hervé Hoppenot from its Board of Directors. Mr. Hoppenot has been a board member since 2014 and previously served as Chairman and CEO.

IndividualHervé Hoppenot
RoleBoard Member
Type of ChangeRetired
Effective DateDecember 10, 2025
ReasonNot disclosed
Replacement InfoNot disclosed
Background DetailsFormer Chairman and CEO
Board/Committee Role ChangesNot disclosed

Original SEC Filing: INCYTE CORP [ INCY ] - 8-K - Dec. 12, 2025
